name: Huevos Ranchero Casserole Souffle
recipeIngredient: 6 Bell peppers (about 6 inches long), use pickled jalapenos that have been rinsed and prepared the same way
2 cups Fresh or frozen corn kernels
2 Fresh jalapeno peppers with seeds and membranes removed
6 cups Purchased or homemade salsa warmed
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F.
2. Butter a 9-by-13-inch oven-proof glass or earthenware casserole dish.
3. Make an incision along the length of each bell pepper and remove the stem, seeds, and membranes, keeping the peppers whole.
4. Fill a medium-size saucepan or skillet halfway with water and bring to a boil. Add the peppers, let the water return to a boil, and cook until the peppers are just softened, about 3 minutes. Remove and blot them very dry with paper towels. Let them cool completely.
5. Line the bottom of the casserole dish with the cooled peppers.
6. In a large bowl, beat the yolks until smooth. Stir in the cheese, corn, milk, jalapeno peppers, salt, and black pepper.
7. In another large bowl, whisk the whites until soft peaks form. Fold them gently into the yolk mixture, stirring just until almost blended.
8. Scrape the batter into the prepared dish.
9. Transfer to the middle rack of the preheated oven and bake for 7 minutes or until the eggs are puffy and the top is lightly browned.
10. Reduce heat to 325°F and continue cooking until the eggs are baked through but not dry, about 22-25 minutes. Test by inserting a knife into the center; it should come out almost clean.
11. Remove from oven and let sit for a few minutes before cutting the casserole into 12 rectangles. Serve each portion with some salsa spooned on top.
